note: Crafts of The Given Land/The Pattern Reach (concrete_abstract axis). THINGCRAFT is the concrete-pole craft of the Masons of the Given — only what can be touched is real; the world's great builders and its great skeptics. FORMCRAFT is the abstract-pole craft of the Figurists — pattern, symbol and idea, who hold matter to be the coarsest layer of the real. This is the axis the GM's own narrative REGISTER rides on (SNG-048): concrete places speak plainly, abstract places earn their poetry. Authored by Aevi, SNG-054.

abilities.name: Thingcraft
abilities.narrationHints: He puts his hand on the stone and the stone tells him the truth, because stone does not do anything else.
abilities.description: The concrete-pole mastery: the real is what you can touch, weigh, and set. The world's finest building and its most stubborn skepticism, in one craft.
abilities.notFor: Symbol, glamour, and argument (Formcraft's country); anything that requires believing in what cannot be weighed.

tree.name: The Weighed Thing
tree.grants: Know a thing's true weight, material, soundness and worth by touch — no lie of appearance survives your hand.
tree.cannot: It tells you what a thing IS, never what it means or what it is for.

tree.name: The Set Stone
tree.grants: Set a thing so it STAYS — a wall, a joint, a boundary, a claim. What you set does not shift, sag, or come apart.
tree.cannot: What is set is hard to un-set, including by you, including when you were wrong to set it.

tree.name: The Denial
tree.grants: Deny an abstraction purchase on the real: illusion, glamour, symbol-working and figure all simply fail in your presence. The thing is what it is.
tree.cannot: It denies the abstract INDISCRIMINATELY — including meanings you needed, including a Figurist ally's working, including hope.

abilities.name: Stone-Read
abilities.narrationHints: The material gives up its history under his palm.
abilities.description: Read a material's truth by touch — the first gift of the Given.
abilities.notFor: Reading meaning or intent; anything not physically present.

tree.name: Stone-Read
tree.grants: Know what a material truly is, and whether it is sound.
tree.cannot: Materials, not makers. It says the beam will fail; not who cut it badly.

tree.name: The Long Grain
tree.grants: Read a thing's history in its material — the stresses it took, the repairs, the day it nearly broke.
tree.cannot: History, not future. It reports what happened, not what happens next.

tree.name: The Given Word
tree.grants: The material of a whole structure is legible at once — every fault, every honest joint, every lie the builder told.
tree.cannot: Buildings you love turn out to be full of lies. The Masons say this is why they are hard to live with.

abilities.name: The Plain Fact
abilities.narrationHints: He says the flat thing and the beautiful argument in the room dies of it.
abilities.description: Reduce a soaring abstraction to the plain touchable fact under it — and everyone present sees the fact.
abilities.notFor: Anything that is genuinely NOT reducible to a touchable fact. Some things aren't, and the Masons' great blindness is not believing that.

tree.name: The Plain Fact
tree.grants: Cut through a rhetorical or symbolic working to the concrete fact beneath, out loud, and make it land.
tree.cannot: Some abstractions are TRUE. Reducing them does not unmake them; it only makes you look small when it fails.

tree.name: The Bedrock
tree.grants: Reduce an argument, a glamour, or a doctrine to its physical basis — and if it has none, that becomes visible to all.
tree.cannot: A doctrine with no physical basis is not thereby false. The Masons lose this argument to the Figurists roughly every generation.

tree.name: The Only Real
tree.grants: In your presence the touchable simply IS the real, and abstraction cannot get purchase — no symbol acts, no figure walks.
tree.cannot: You have made a world where nothing means anything. Some people find that a relief, and the ones who do worry the Masons most.

abilities.name: Formcraft
abilities.narrationHints: She draws a shape that is not a picture of anything, and the room rearranges itself to agree with it.
abilities.description: The abstract-pole mastery: pattern, symbol and idea as the FINER layer of the real. To a Figurist, matter is the coarse residue of form.
abilities.notFor: Anything that requires the touchable to be primary; the Masons' Denial defeats it outright.

tree.name: The Pattern Eye
tree.grants: See the pattern, symbol, or structure a thing is an instance OF — the form behind the fact.
tree.cannot: Seeing a form does not give you power over its instance. Not yet.

tree.name: The Figure
tree.grants: Make a symbol that ACTS — a mark, a shape, a figure that does in the world what it means.
tree.cannot: A figure does what it MEANS, not what you wanted it to mean. Be precise, or be surprised.

tree.name: The Argument Made Real
tree.grants: Build an idea into the world so completely that it becomes a fact others must move around — a rule, a boundary, a truth-in-effect.
tree.cannot: The Masons can deny it. Reality can, eventually, decline. And a made truth that was never true rots from the inside.

abilities.name: Pattern-Sense
abilities.narrationHints: The repetition surfaces — this has a shape, and the shape has a name.
abilities.description: See the pattern beneath particulars — the first gift of the Pattern Reach.
abilities.notFor: Reading material (Stone-Read); reading order-as-structure (Order-Sense). This reads FORM.

tree.name: Pattern-Sense
tree.grants: See that a set of particulars is an instance of a pattern, and roughly what pattern.
tree.cannot: Seeing the pattern is not seeing whether it is a true one. Figurists find patterns in noise, constantly.

tree.name: The Named Form
tree.grants: Name the form exactly — and once named, its other instances become visible to you everywhere.
tree.cannot: Once you can see it everywhere, you will see it where it is not.

tree.name: The Formal Reach
tree.grants: Read the form of an event, a life, or a history — the shape it is completing.
tree.cannot: Knowing the shape a life is completing does not permit you to change it, and it is a terrible thing to carry.

abilities.name: The Walking Figure
abilities.narrationHints: The mark she drew stands up off the wall and goes to do the thing it means.
abilities.description: Send a made figure to act at a distance — a symbol given errand and enough form to run it.
abilities.notFor: Making a figure that means what you did not mean; the figure will do the second thing, not the first.

tree.name: The Walking Figure
tree.grants: Send a figure to carry a meaning somewhere — a warning, a ward, a binding — and it arrives and acts.
tree.cannot: It acts once and it is spent. And it acts on what it MEANS.

tree.name: The Standing Figure
tree.grants: The figure persists — it keeps meaning, keeps acting, keeps the ward or the rule in force after you have gone.
tree.cannot: A standing figure is a rule nobody is supervising. It will apply itself to cases you never imagined.

tree.name: The Figure That Learns
tree.grants: The figure adapts its meaning to circumstance — it keeps doing what you MEANT even where you did not foresee.
tree.cannot: Now it is deciding what you meant. The Figurists' oldest disasters all begin with a figure that was quite sure it understood.

abilities.name: Sound Repair
abilities.narrationHints: Not better. Sound. The thing as it was, correctly made, correctly set.
abilities.description: Fix what is broken by understanding its material nature — what it is, what it was, and what it takes to make it sound again.

tree.name: Correct Fix
tree.grants: Repair what is broken by understanding what it truly is — the right material intervention, no more and no less, that makes the thing sound again.

tree.name: Sound Again
tree.grants: Restore not just function but structural integrity — the wall that will not sag, the wound that will not fester, the joint that will hold under load.

tree.name: As It Was
tree.grants: Return a thing fully to its proper material state — the building, the body, the system — so that it is indistinguishable from one that was never broken.

abilities.name: The Set Word
abilities.narrationHints: A mason's word is set the way a stone is set. It does not shift.
abilities.description: What the mason says stands. Not persuasion — the plain authority of someone whose word is as solid as their work.

tree.name: Plain Statement
tree.grants: Say the plain thing and have it land with authority — not eloquence, just solidity. The practical fact that settles an argument by being undeniable.

tree.name: The Word That Holds
tree.grants: Make an agreement or declaration that sticks — not because you charmed anyone, but because the weight of your standing and your history of keeping your word makes it so.

tree.name: Set
tree.grants: Name the practical truth of a situation so plainly and with such authority that people organize around it as if it were the wall they're building against.

abilities.name: The Near Way
abilities.narrationHints: The seam is already there. He just finds it.
abilities.description: Navigate through built or material spaces by understanding how they're made — the door that's in every wall, the gap that's in every structure.

tree.name: The Seam
tree.grants: Find the gap, the loose stone, the door that's always in the structure — the mason knows how things are built and knows where they open.

tree.name: Material Passage
tree.grants: Move through a built environment at the speed its construction allows — the route that doesn't backtrack, the way that's already there in the material.

tree.name: The Way In
tree.grants: In any built structure, find or make the passage. Not by brute force — by knowing exactly where the material wants to open.

abilities.name: The Long Haul
abilities.narrationHints: Lay the next stone. Then the one after that. The wall goes up.
abilities.description: Endure through material steadiness — not inspiration, not second winds, just the next thing done correctly until the work is complete.

tree.name: Just Keep Going
tree.grants: Work past fatigue, cold, hunger, or pain by not stopping — the mason's endurance is the next correct action taken, not inspiration.

tree.name: The Steady Pace
tree.grants: Sustain useful work across a long haul without the degradation that hits others — the quality doesn't fall, the pace doesn't drop, the hand stays true.

tree.name: Until It's Done
tree.grants: Continue until the work is finished, regardless of what the body says. The task completes. This is simply what you do.

abilities.name: The Restored Form
abilities.narrationHints: Correct the form and the thing follows. The pattern is what was broken.
abilities.description: Restore by identifying the correct form and reinstating it — not by nursing the wound but by re-establishing the pattern that was disrupted.

tree.name: The Correct Pattern
tree.grants: Identify the form a broken thing is a disruption of, and name the intervention that restores the pattern — not approximate, but correct.

tree.name: Form Before Function
tree.grants: Restore the underlying structure — the form a person, plan, or object is supposed to have — and function follows without separate work.

tree.name: Full Re-Instantiation
tree.grants: Return a thing entirely to its proper form — the body that heals from the inside out, the relationship that finds its right shape, the plan that coheres again — by working at the level of pattern rather than symptom.

abilities.name: The Warding Mark
abilities.narrationHints: She drew a mark that means NOT HERE. The thing reads it correctly.
abilities.description: A figure drawn against something keeps it back. The mark is a statement about what belongs and what doesn't.

tree.name: The Mark Against
tree.grants: Draw or declare a figure that keeps a specific category of thing — an intent, a person, a working — from entering or acting in a space.

tree.name: The Persistent Ward
tree.grants: Set a warding figure that holds without your continued attention — it knows what it's keeping back and it keeps it back until the mark is broken.

tree.name: The Absolute Exclusion
tree.grants: Name what does not belong here with such formal clarity that it cannot be here — the working fails, the intent dissolves, the thing that should not be cannot maintain form in the space you've marked.

abilities.name: The Patterned Way
abilities.narrationHints: A landscape has a shape. Move along its lines.
abilities.description: Navigate by reading the pattern a space is — not terrain but form; not where the road goes but what the space is an instance of.

tree.name: The Shape of a Place
tree.grants: Read a landscape or structure as a pattern — see where it opens, where it funnels, where it repeats — and move along its lines rather than across them.

tree.name: Pattern Navigation
tree.grants: Navigate any environment you can read the form of without needing roads, signs, or memory — the pattern tells you where you are and where leads where.

tree.name: This Place Is an Instance
tree.grants: Recognize that a place is an instance of a form you know — and everything about it becomes legible: every exit, every blind spot, every place the pattern says something must be.

abilities.name: The Held Form
abilities.narrationHints: Keep the form alive and what it represents persists.
abilities.description: Sustain something by maintaining its correct form in your mind — the pattern held is the thing held.

tree.name: Form Maintenance
tree.grants: Hold a pattern clearly in mind and the thing it represents persists longer than it otherwise would — an agreement, a working, a person's coherence under pressure.

tree.name: The Sustained Pattern
tree.grants: Keep the form of something alive across time and distance — a relationship, a plan, a person's sense of themselves — by maintaining its correct representation.

tree.name: As Long as the Form Holds
tree.grants: While you hold the form of something perfectly in your mind, it does not cease — the pattern is the sustaining principle, and you are the one sustaining it.

abilities.name: The Binding Figure
abilities.narrationHints: You draw the figure and the thing it describes becomes bound to obey it — the map, for a while, ruling the territory.
abilities.description: The Figurist art of the binding sign — a figure drawn so true that reality briefly consents to match it, the abstract pattern given authority over the concrete thing. To draw it right is to legislate, briefly, what is.
abilities.notFor: It binds through the figure's truth, so a false or sloppy figure binds nothing — and the world only consents for a while. Reality always resumes being itself; the figure rules until it doesn't.

tree.name: The Binding Figure
tree.grants: Draw a binding figure that compels a concrete thing to match its abstract pattern for a time.
tree.cannot: It fails if the figure is untrue, it holds only for a while before reality resumes, and a figure that binds the world binds you to its upkeep while it lasts.

abilities.name: The True Name
abilities.narrationHints: You find the one figure that is not a description of the thing but the thing's own name — and a thing called by its true name has no choice but to turn and answer.
abilities.description: The Figurist capstone — the true figure, the sign that IS the thing rather than describes it. To know a thing's true name is to hold a handle on its very being. The deepest and most dangerous Figurist working, because names cut both ways.
abilities.notFor: One true name, found at enormous cost, and it must be TRUE — a guessed name binds nothing and warns the thing you tried. And to speak a true name is to be known by it in turn: the naming runs both directions.

tree.name: The True Name
tree.grants: Find and speak a thing's true name, gaining a hold on its very being.
tree.cannot: A false name fails and forewarns, the finding costs nearly all you have, and to name truly is to be truly named — you cannot hold a thing's name without offering your own.

abilities.name: The Keystone Blow
abilities.narrationHints: You do not batter the wall. You find the one stone the whole thing is truly resting on, and you strike THAT, and the wall discovers it was only ever an agreement between stones.
abilities.description: The Mason art of the keystone blow — reading a made structure to its single load-bearing truth and striking exactly there, so the whole comes down for the cost of one perfect blow. To break well is to understand first.
abilities.notFor: It breaks the made, not the living or the grown — and it needs a real keystone; some things bear their load everywhere at once and have no single stone to strike. Read wrong and you have merely hit a wall.

tree.name: The Keystone Blow
tree.grants: Find and strike a structure's single load-bearing point, bringing the whole down with one blow.
tree.cannot: It works only on made structures with a true keystone, it fails on distributed loads, and a thing brought down cannot be un-broken — you understood it in order to end it.

abilities.name: The Unmaking of Walls
abilities.narrationHints: You put your hand flat against the made thing and remember it, stone by stone, back to the un-built quarry it came from — and it goes, willingly almost, back to being nothing that was ever a wall.
abilities.description: The Mason capstone — the unmaking of a made thing back to its raw un-built state, a whole edifice returned to stone and dust as if it had never risen. The great and grieving gift of those who know building best.
abilities.notFor: One structure, once, at enormous cost — it unmakes only what was made, never the grown or living, and it cannot be reversed; an unmade wall does not remember how to be a wall. Those who build best grieve this most.

tree.name: The Unmaking of Walls
tree.grants: Unmake a whole built structure back to its raw, un-built material.
tree.cannot: It touches only the made, it costs nearly all you have, and it CANNOT be undone — the ones who understand building are the ones who feel the unmaking most.
